title: Workshop: Architect the Heat-Relief Information Hub
summary: Workshop: Architect the Heat-Relief Information Hub uses an original information-architecture wall scenario to retrieve earlier HTML skills, add a bounded artifact layer, and verify transfer with source and browser evidence.
objectives: Retrieve html document boilerplate, html elements nesting, html text headings lists without step-by-step imitation.
Produce and verify a real information-architecture wall artifact or evidence report.
Preserve earlier valid requirements while diagnosing changed content and constraints.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Build the public information artifact here. -->

Information-architecture wall case 3: every sentence should be a heading because headings receive stronger default styling.

steps.title: Rehearse checkpoint 8 for the information-architecture wall · workshop
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 8: Put this verification cycle in the order that exposes the earliest source failure. Work context: information-architecture wall.
steps.why: Workshop: Architect the Heat-Relief Information Hub depends on causal ordering because each decision must preserve document meaning, user control, portability, and inspectable evidence. This is checkpoint 8 in the information-architecture wall.

content.text: Reconstruct the information-architecture wall sequence by dependency, not by how familiar each term looks.

hints: Workshop: Architect the Heat-Relief Information Hub: first isolate the evidence required by “Rehearse checkpoint 8 for the information-architecture wall.”

options.text: information-architecture wall phase 8.1: choose an element that represents the outer relationship
information-architecture wall phase 8.2: open the parent before its children
information-architecture wall phase 8.3: complete and close each child in order
information-architecture wall phase 8.4: close the parent after its final child
